nts1.n: PUM PUM POWER HOUR W/ LIL C
nts1.d:
Party starter & dancehall connoisseur Lil C live in session.
From the host: "Business as usual : bringing you a sexy selection of contemporary afro-carribean pop music , lovingly curated by lil c"

Founders of Lagos’ new-age underground festival, FREEWATER, join NTS for a 6-show residency, diving deep into the next wave of Nigerian music. Each hour-long show explores our favorite jams from the city’s bubbling alternative scene, covering alt-afro, indie, electronic, and trap sounds shaping the future of Lagos’ underground.
DJ and producer, Architect, blends Rap music of all flavours and forms. Connecting classic and contemporary sounds from the UK, US, Caribbean and beyond.
D.C R&B royalty Ari Lennox in the London studio with Selassie.

Charisse C takes us on a monthly journey through an electronic soundscape birthed by the melting pot which raised her, anchored by South African giants Amapiano & Gqom, featuring soulful, bass-heavy & playful selections.
Ruf Dug brings the leftfield: old video game soundtracks, dusty analogue dreamers and poolside Balearic sounds. No nonsense from the original tropical cyberpunk.

Brooklyn, NY born and based DJ and record producer Dazegxd spends an hour on the NTS waves with an exhibition of his signature style - blending cuts dancy, deep, and downright classic from hip-hop to jungle.
From the host: "First 20mins of this mix are brand new and the other 40mins is a recording of my set @ Le Popup Du Label in Paris, France (ft. Muddyoush & the Delusion Records Crew on the mic). Expect bare wheelups on the second half i was feelin myself lol"

nts1.n: ED BANGER W/ BUSY P, MYD, AND TATYANA JANE
nts1.d: Ed Banger bringing the party to the London studio in a two hour session with the big wig Busy P and label stalwart Myd and Tatyana Jane.
Nocturnal moods from the haze of the billiards hall with Diogo & friends…

nts1.n: NTS GUIDE TO: INDEPENDENT NORTH AMERICAN JAZZ VOL 4 (R)
nts1.d: This guide takes another deep dive into the world of independently released and private-press jazz from across the US and Canada. The fourth instalment highlights visionary artists who worked beyond the major label system: from the spiritual minimalism of Ahmed Jamal and the cosmic brass meditations of Brother Ah, to the rare community recording of The African Jazz Ensemble, captured live at Brightwood Elementary School in Washington D.C. Expect modal explorations, small-press ingenuity, and the restless experimentation that defined the continent’s underground jazz movement.
Ziggy Devriendt is the DJ behind Nosedrip. Tune in once a month for an expert selection of any or all of the following: new age, new wave, French psych, minimal synth, post-punk and a whole lot else…

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Japanese '70s Folk Special (R)
THE ONE GLOVE BREAKFAST SHOW W/ MACCA
THE EXTENDED PLAY SESSIONS W/ MR PEDRO
DIDDY WAH w/ Gabriel Olivier Hernandez

nts2.d: Carolina Soul is a record seller based in Durham, NC, USA. They specialize in rare and classic soul, boogie, gospel and modern obscure tracks from the Carolinas and beyond, mixed with all-time bangers and ballads.

The Hungarian musician, soundtrack composer and actor Mihály Vig, most famous for his film scores for Béla Tarr, and member of the bands Balaton and Trabant, stops by the NTS studio to play some favourites. From Chuck Berry to Mozart.
The South London postpunk crew take control sharing an hour of selections every month.

Olivia O. — musician, multidisciplinary artist, performing under Lowertown, Olivia O., and child star — delivers a set blending cult discoveries, current obsessions, and deep cuts from her collaborators and the NYC underground.
Lesser heard sounds with a focus on femmes in punk from artist and musician Sara A.

nts2.n: High Noon: Land Of Broken Dreams w/ Dina J
nts2.d:
Dina Juntila aka Dina J is a Los Angeles based DJ. record collector and music supervisor. "High Noon" is a seated round about of sounds with the idea that the mix is its own OST for some unknown film. Each month features a special guest with a similar sickness.
From the host: "The cry of 'Flower Power' echoes through the land. We shall not wilt. Let a thousand flowers bloom. — Abbie Hoffman"

nts2.n: MACHINES OF LOVING GRACE W/ ULRIKA SPACEK
nts2.d:
Midwife’s multi-instrumentalist and recording engineer Madeline Johnston presents “Machines of Loving Grace,” a monthly transmission featuring her new finds and old favorites with a focus on the golden age of DIY community, cosmic sentimentalism, world music, and experimental pop. “Machines of Loving Grace” is a gateway into Johnston’s process and personal soundtrack.
Inspired by the Richard Brautigan poem, Midwife’s curated playlists explore the idea of “a cybernetic ecology;” acoustic and electronic sources reflect on our humanity as well as our animalistic nature in the context of a digital landscape.

Camella Lobo, aka Tropic of Cancer, takes to the air once a month for Cry Later. Tune in for a dedication hour of songs selected by listeners in honor of their lovers and loved ones. Send a personal message and song selection to cryinglater@gmail.com
From the host: "In honor of all the vovós - a selection of songs from the women on Brazilian airwaves between 1957 and 1994 to celebrate fierce dedication to love and family. Feliz aniversário, pai."

nts2.n: Bump City w/ Billy Goods & Yamagucci
nts2.d:
Funkmosphere resident Billy Goods heads up a 2 hour slot with James Yamagucci - all vinyl, all live and direct from our LA studio.
From the host: "BUMP CITY 062 Mix w/ DJ's Billy Goods & Yamagucci laying down 2 hours of sophisticated soul, underground house and RNB Nujazz."

nts2.n: ENERGY RESERVE SYSTEMS w/ KITTY CA$H
nts2.d: Kitty Ca$h presents Energy Reserve Systems – regular high voltage journeys through deep house, club edits, and experimental textures to reclabirate your spirit.

nts2.n: Vibrations for a Better World w/ AOKI takamasa
skate muzik: Sovereign Selections w/ Altered States Tapes
ONO w/ JC Leisure Group - Low Tide, Hi Grypus!

subtle.n: Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

subtle.n: 5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

dublab.n: Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.

McAllister and Kyle Art Threat - Please and Thank You

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy
